devdoc.author: Dark Pool Protocol
devdoc.details: Uses 15 distinct FHE operations: ge, min, sub, mul, add, select, randEuint64,      eq (x2), gt, makePubliclyDecryptable, asEaddress, asEuint64, allowTransient, and.      SELL orders: maker deposits ETH, taker pays USDC.      BUY orders: maker deposits USDC, taker pays ETH.

cancelOrder(uint256).details: SELL orders refund remaining ETH; BUY orders refund remaining USDC.

params.orderId: The ID of the order to cancel

params._paymentToken: The ERC20 token address used for escrow (e.g., USDC)

createOrder(bytes32,bytes,bytes32,bytes,.details: SELL orders: maker deposits ETH via msg.value (sells ETH for USDC).      BUY orders: maker deposits USDC via approve+transferFrom (buys ETH with USDC).      Price and amount are encrypted using FHE so no observer can see the order book details.      FHE operations: fromExternal (x2), allowThis (x3), allow (x2), asEaddress

params.amountProof: ZK proof for the encrypted amount
params.isBuy: Whether this is a buy or sell order
params.priceProof: ZK proof for the encrypted price
params.usdcDeposit: The plaintext amount of USDC to escrow (BUY orders only, 0 for SELL)

returns.orderId: The ID of the newly created order

fillOrder(uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes32,.details: This is the core dark pool matching engine. The function is split across      internal helpers to avoid stack-too-deep. See _computeFill and _recordFill.      For SELL orders (maker deposited ETH): taker provides USDC, receives ETH.      For BUY orders (maker deposited USDC): taker provides ETH (msg.value), receives USDC.      FHE operations used (15 total):        1. FHE.ge        - Encrypted price comparison        2. FHE.min       - Partial fill calculation        3. FHE.asEuint64 - Create encrypted zero constant        4. FHE.select    - Conditional fill (encrypted ternary)        5. FHE.mul       - Encrypted settlement total        6. FHE.sub       - Update remaining quantity        7. FHE.select    - Conditional remaining update        8. FHE.eq        - Check if fully filled        9. FHE.gt        - Check if fill is positive       10. FHE.and       - Compound boolean logic       11. FHE.randEuint64 - Fair tiebreaking randomness       12. FHE.asEaddress  - Encrypt taker counterparty       13. FHE.add         - Accumulate protocol volume       14. FHE.makePubliclyDecryptable - Post-trade transparency       15. FHE.eq         - Amount consistency verification       +   FHE.allowTransient - Gas-optimized transient ACL

params.orderId: The ID of the order to fill
params.takerAmountProof: ZK proof for the taker's encrypted amount
params.takerEthAmount: ETH the taker wants (SELL) or provides (BUY) - plaintext
params.takerPriceProof: ZK proof for the taker's encrypted price
params.takerUsdcAmount: USDC the taker provides (SELL) or wants (BUY) - plaintext

params.orderId: The order to grant access for
params.viewer: The address to grant view access to

params.orderId: The order to grant auditor access to

devdoc.title: ConfidentialOTC - Confidential Dark Pool with Deep FHE Usage

SenderNotAllowedToUseHandle(bytes32,addr.notice: Returned if the sender is not allowed to use the handle.

ZamaProtocolUnsupported().notice: Returned if the Zama protocol is not supported on the current chain

AccessGranted(uint256,address).notice: Emitted when a maker grants view access to a third party

AuditorAccessGranted(uint256).notice: Emitted when auditor is granted access to an order

AuditorUpdated(address,address).notice: Emitted when the auditor address is updated

FillVolumePublished(uint256).notice: Emitted when fill volume is made publicly decryptable

OrderCancelled(uint256,uint256,uint256).notice: Emitted when an order is cancelled and assets refunded

OrderCreated(uint256,address,string,bool.notice: Emitted when a new order is created with escrow

OrderFilled(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint.notice: Emitted when an order is partially or fully filled

OwnershipTransferred(address,address).notice: Emitted when contract ownership is transferred

auditor().notice: Compliance auditor who can be granted access to any order/fill

cancelOrder(uint256).notice: Cancel an open order and refund the escrowed assets to the maker

constructor.notice: Deploys the dark pool and sets the deployer as owner

createOrder(bytes32,bytes,bytes32,bytes,.notice: Create an OTC order with encrypted price and amount, depositing escrow

fillCount().notice: Returns the total number of fills executed

fillOrder(uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes32,.notice: Fill an open order with encrypted price matching and two-sided asset transfer

getEncryptedTaker(uint256).notice: Get the encrypted taker address handle for an order

getFill(uint256).notice: Get public fields of a fill

getFillAmount(uint256).notice: Get the encrypted fill amount handle

getFillEncryptedTaker(uint256).notice: Get the encrypted taker address from a fill

getFillPriorityScore(uint256).notice: Get the encrypted priority score for fair tiebreaking

getFillTotal(uint256).notice: Get the encrypted settlement total handle

getMyFills().notice: Get the list of fill IDs where the caller was the taker

getOrder(uint256).notice: Get public (non-encrypted) fields of an order

getOrderFills(uint256).notice: Get the list of fill IDs for a given order

getRemainingAmount(uint256).notice: Get the encrypted remaining amount handle

getTotalVolume().notice: Get the encrypted total protocol volume handle

grantAccess(uint256,address).notice: Maker grants a specific address permission to decrypt order fields

grantAuditorAccess(uint256).notice: Grant the auditor access to decrypt all fields of an order and its fills

orderCount().notice: Returns the total number of orders created

paymentToken().notice: ERC20 payment token used for escrow (e.g., USDC)

setAuditor(address).notice: Set the compliance auditor address (owner only)

userdoc.notice: A fully encrypted OTC dark pool where prices, amounts, and counterparties are         hidden using Fully Homomorphic Encryption. Supports two-sided ETH/USDC swaps,         encrypted price matching, partial fills, encrypted settlement totals, fair         tiebreaking via FHE randomness, encrypted counterparty addresses,         compliance/auditor access, and post-trade transparency for fill volumes.
